note: Generated by `cargo xtask cli-help`. Read this file to understand all CLI commands before updating README.md or writing documentation.

commands.help:
Git-native secrets manager

Usage: gitvault [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  encrypt       Encrypt a secret file
  decrypt       Decrypt a .age encrypted file
  materialize   Materialize secrets to root .env
  status        Check repository safety status
  harden        Harden repository (update .gitignore, install hooks)
  run           Run a command with secrets injected as environment variables
  allow-prod    Write a timed production allow token
  merge-driver  Run as git merge driver for .env files Usage: git config merge.gitvault-env.driver "gitvault merge-driver %O %A %B"
  recipient     Manage persistent recipients
  rotate        Re-encrypt all secrets with the current recipients list
  keyring       Manage identity key in OS keyring
  check         Run preflight validation without side effects
  revoke-prod   Revoke the production allow token immediately
  identity      Manage local identity keys
  ai            AI tooling helpers (skill and context print)
  help          Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help
  -V, --version
          Print version

Encrypt a secret file

Usage: gitvault encrypt [OPTIONS] <FILE>

Arguments:
  <FILE>  File to encrypt

Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
  -r, --recipient <RECIPIENTS>
          Recipient age public key (repeat for multi-recipient; defaults to local identity if omitted)
      --keep-path
          Preserve input path relative to repo root under secrets/<env>/
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --fields <FIELDS>
          Fields to encrypt (comma-separated key paths, for JSON/YAML/TOML field-level encryption)
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --value-only
          Encrypt .env values individually instead of whole-file
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help

Decrypt a .age encrypted file

Usage: gitvault decrypt [OPTIONS] <FILE>

Arguments:
  <FILE>  Encrypted .age file to decrypt

Options:
  -i, --identity <IDENTITY>
          Identity key file path (or use `GITVAULT_IDENTITY` env var)
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
  -o, --output [<OUTPUT>]
          Output file path (default: strip .age extension)
      --fields <FIELDS>
          Fields to decrypt (comma-separated key paths, for JSON/YAML/TOML)
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --reveal
          Print decrypted content to stdout instead of writing to file
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
      --value-only
          Decrypt .env values individually (reverse of --value-only encrypt)
  -h, --help
          Print help

Materialize secrets to root .env

Usage: gitvault materialize [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -e, --env <ENV>
          Environment to use (overrides `SECRETS_ENV` and .secrets/env)
      --json
          Output results as JSON
  -i, --identity <IDENTITY>
          Identity key file path
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --prod
          Require production barrier for prod env
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help

Check repository safety status

Usage: gitvault status [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --fail-if-dirty
          Fail with exit code 6 if secrets/ has uncommitted changes
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help

Harden repository (update .gitignore, install hooks)

Usage: gitvault harden [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help

Run a command with secrets injected as environment variables

Usage: gitvault run [OPTIONS] -- <COMMAND>...

Arguments:
  <COMMAND>...  Command and arguments to run

Options:
  -e, --env <ENV>
          Environment to use
      --json
          Output results as JSON
  -i, --identity <IDENTITY>
          Identity key file path (or use `GITVAULT_IDENTITY` env var)
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --prod
          Require production barrier (required when env=prod)
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --clear-env
          Start child with empty environment
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
      --pass <VARS>
          Comma-separated vars to pass through when --clear-env is set
  -h, --help
          Print help

Write a timed production allow token

Usage: gitvault allow-prod [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --ttl <TTL>
          Token lifetime in seconds [default: 3600]
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help

Run as git merge driver for .env files Usage: git config merge.gitvault-env.driver "gitvault merge-driver %O %A %B"

Usage: gitvault merge-driver [OPTIONS] <BASE> <OURS> <THEIRS>

Arguments:
  <BASE>    Base version (ancestor)
  <OURS>    Ours version (current branch, will be overwritten with merge result)
  <THEIRS>  Theirs version (incoming branch)

Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help

Manage persistent recipients

Usage: gitvault recipient [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  add     Add a recipient public key
  remove  Remove a recipient public key
  list    List current recipients
  help    Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help

Manage identity key in OS keyring

Usage: gitvault keyring [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  set     Store identity key in OS keyring
  get     Show public key of stored identity
  delete  Remove stored identity from OS keyring
  help    Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help

Manage local identity keys

Usage: gitvault identity [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  create  Create a new identity key
  help    Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>

          Print help

Create a new identity key

Usage: gitvault identity create [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --profile <PROFILE>
          Identity profile: classic (age X25519) or hybrid (age X25519 + future PQ-ready label) [default: classic] [possible values: classic, hybrid]
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --out <PATH>
          Export identity to file (optional; default: store in OS keyring)
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help

AI tooling helpers (skill and context print)

Usage: gitvault ai [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  skill    Print canonical gitvault skill content for Copilot usage
  context  Print concise project AI context for agent onboarding
  help     Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help
